jokes.text: They cut his face off. And they made it into a dream catcher. And they made his legs into rain sticks. And that's the great thing about Indians back then--is they used every part of the pioneer.
jokes.setup: Historical account of a pioneer's murder by Native Americans
jokes.punchline: Cheerful observation about efficiency, treating human body parts like buffalo
jokes.explanation: Dark historical content delivered with Leslie's characteristic earnest optimism creates brilliant cognitive dissonance. The 'used every part' parallel is clever and unexpected.

jokes.text: Sorry, is a great man. I, for one, am glad that they're keeping him alive.
jokes.setup: Leslie corrects herself about Tony Tellenson being a great man
jokes.punchline: Implies he's being artificially kept alive against nature
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Leslie's political correctness taken too far, but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: Like the Kennedys or the Bushes. Minus the drinking problem. I mean no disrespect.
jokes.setup: Comparing Knope family to political dynasties
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges their alcoholism then immediately apologizes
jokes.explanation: Leslie's overly diplomatic nature is on display, but the joke is fairly predictable.

jokes.text: In 1994, I gave her a nickname. It's unrepeatable, but it stuck. It's my proudest accomplishment.
jokes.setup: Ron talking about giving Marlene a nickname
jokes.punchline: His proudest accomplishment is an insult nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - his pride in such a petty accomplishment is very in character.

jokes.text: It's the Iron [bleep] of Pawnee.
jokes.setup: Ron revealing his proudest accomplishment nickname
jokes.punchline: The censored profanity combined with 'Iron Lady' reference
jokes.explanation: Pays off the setup perfectly with a memorable nickname that plays on Iron Lady.

jokes.text: Oh, it didn't answer your call? Maybe because it was in the Smithsonian.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking rhetorically about the importance of speeches
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of asking the Gettysburg Address
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to over-elaborate on rhetorical questions, but execution is clunky.

jokes.text: I was learning disabled, and my mom cast a very long shadow.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Donna to read back her dictation
jokes.punchline: Donna has misheard and transcribed something completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Straightforward mishearing gag, gets the cringe but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Marlene Griggs-Knope is morbidly obese.
jokes.setup: Another transcription error after Leslie corrects the first one
jokes.punchline: An even worse mishearing that's more offensive
jokes.explanation: Escalates the mishearing gag but relies too heavily on just being more offensive.

jokes.text: Abraham Lincoln, when he started his speeches, he would come up and say, 'My name's Abraham Lincoln. I'm the President of the United States. But I'm gonna be honest with you. I have no clue what I'm doing.'
jokes.setup: Mark giving Leslie speech advice about appearing humble
jokes.punchline: Completely fabricated Lincoln quote that's anachronistically casual
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Lincoln's gravitas and modern casual speech, but Mark isn't fully developed as character yet.

jokes.text: Marlene Griggs-Knope is definitely not a whore. Marlene Griggs-Knope has not five, but seven Asian friends. Marlene Griggs-Knope has never solicited a male prostitute. Marlene Griggs-Knope has said the 'N' word only four times in her entire life.
jokes.setup: Mark trying to help with speech content
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate 'compliments' that would be terrible to say publicly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure but feels like generic 'offensive suggestions' rather than character-specific humor.

jokes.text: She said it more than that?
jokes.setup: Leslie rejecting his inappropriate speech suggestions
jokes.punchline: Mark misinterprets her rejection as meaning the number was too low
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection beat but character feels generic rather than specific to Mark.

jokes.text: Well, 'cause I would feel a little silly putting on a nice dress to go to the couch.
jokes.setup: Andy asking why Ann doesn't dress up for him
jokes.punchline: Their social life is just sitting on the couch
jokes.explanation: Relatable relationship humor that establishes their dynamic well.

jokes.text: Babe, I'm out of milk.
jokes.setup: Ann talking about how excited she is for a magical evening out
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately brings her back to mundane reality
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation and character moment for Andy's obliviousness.

jokes.text: The Tellensons is like the Oscars times the Grammys plus the Super Bowl.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing the importance of the local government banquet
jokes.punchline: Wildly inflated comparison for a small-town government event
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her tendency to over-inflate the importance of government.

jokes.text: He's the guy that made Larry Bird look the way he does.
jokes.setup: Leslie praising the local barber's prestigious clientele
jokes.punchline: Larry Bird was famously not known for good hair
jokes.explanation: Good reference humor that works on multiple levels for those who know Larry Bird.

jokes.text: My feet hurt.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking for the 'inside scoop' from the prestigious barber
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane, non-insider response
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Leslie's expectations with economy and timing.

jokes.text: What the hell else would you call it?
jokes.setup: Leslie asking if being Marlene's daughter counts as being related
jokes.punchline: Salvatore's blunt response to obvious question
jokes.explanation: Establishes Salvatore as no-nonsense character who deflates Leslie's verbal flourishes.

jokes.text: Salvatore calls this hairdo 'The Mayor.'
jokes.setup: Leslie getting a fancy political haircut
jokes.punchline: The haircut has an official political name
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that plays into Leslie's political aspirations and the show's small-town setting.

jokes.text: And yes, I will wear my hair like this when I am the first female mayor of Pawnee.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off her new mayoral haircut
jokes.punchline: She's already planning to wear it when she becomes mayor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her confidence and long-term political planning.

jokes.text: I just wanted to tell you that I think that what you two are doing is so brave. And just being who you are-- I wish I had your courage.
jokes.setup: Former councilman approaching Leslie and Ann at the formal event
jokes.punchline: He assumes they're a lesbian couple based on their appearance/behavior
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy that creates awkwardness for both characters and audience.

jokes.text: Okay, let's go.
jokes.setup: Frank's assumption about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate desire to escape the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat that pays off the awkward setup.

jokes.text: I enjoy government functions like I enjoy getting kicked in the nuggets with a steel-toe boot. But this hotel always serves bacon-wrapped shrimp. That's my number one favorite food wrapped around my number three favorite food.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he attends government functions he hates
jokes.punchline: Specific food ranking system that overrides his hatred
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson - perfectly captures his hatred of government mixed with his love of meat and specific food preferences.

jokes.text: I'd go to a banquet and honor those Somali pirates if they served bacon-wrapped shrimp.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his devotion to bacon-wrapped shrimp
jokes.punchline: He'd honor contemporary villains for the right food
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows the extent of Ron's food priorities over political principles.

jokes.text: I wasn't offering.
jokes.setup: Ann politely declining bacon-wrapped shrimp
jokes.punchline: Ron clarifies he was just announcing his own acquisition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his selfishness with food delivered with perfect deadpan timing.

jokes.text: It's just like my brother's. He's an officer in the Air Force.
jokes.setup: Ron commenting on Leslie's fancy new haircut
jokes.punchline: It looks like a military man's haircut
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback that deflates Leslie's feminine political aspirations.

jokes.text: I don't know who that is, that's somebody's wife.
jokes.setup: Leslie identifying the living Tellenson Award winners
jokes.punchline: One person is just identified as an appendage
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about gender dynamics in political circles, fits Leslie's character.

jokes.text: Wait, Oscar Pfortmiller's dead. That's his disappointing son, Theo. Another dynasty.
jokes.setup: Leslie identifying political figures
jokes.punchline: Casually calls the son disappointing while praising dynasties
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's political gossip knowledge and dynasty obsession.

jokes.text: If a bomb went off in here, it would definitely make the Indianapolis papers.
jokes.setup: Leslie marveling at all the important people gathered
jokes.punchline: Measures importance by potential terrorist attack news coverage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that shows Leslie's warped sense of measuring political importance.

jokes.text: Maybe I should mention her massive weight loss.
jokes.setup: Leslie planning to approach Janine Restrepo
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests bringing up something potentially sensitive
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's social awkwardness and tendency to overshare, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, really? Well, I'm Marlene Griggs-Knope and I will destroy you all. [laughter]
jokes.setup: Role-playing exercise getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Tom goes full supervillain as Marlene
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the role-play scene, captures Tom's theatrical nature.

jokes.text: It's fun to pretend to be zoning board members.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking what they're doing with their weird role-play
jokes.punchline: Mark's completely earnest explanation of their bizarre behavior
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of an absurd but somehow reasonable explanation.

jokes.text: Salvatore usually does men.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off her haircut to her mother
jokes.punchline: Marlene's subtle dig at Leslie's masculine-looking haircut
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered maternal criticism disguised as observation.

jokes.text: Yeah, friend. She's--we're friends. Just friends. I have a boyfriend. He's a man.
jokes.setup: Marlene referring to Ann as Leslie's friend
jokes.punchline: Ann over-compensating after the earlier lesbian assumption
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Ann is still rattled by the earlier misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Yes, and it's 22 minutes long. With the song. I could cut the song. The song's cut.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentioning she wrote a speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals absurd length and immediately backtracks on musical element
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment showing her over-preparation and quick adaptability.

jokes.text: I'll make her feel like a human dinner roll.
jokes.setup: Marlene telling Leslie to butter up Janine Restrepo
jokes.punchline: Leslie takes the butter metaphor literally and absurdly
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that shows Leslie's tendency to extend metaphors in unexpected ways.

jokes.text: Marlene Griggs-Knope is my mom. But she's so much more than that. She's also... my mother.
jokes.setup: Leslie beginning her formal speech
jokes.punchline: She distinguishes between 'mom' and 'mother' as if they're different
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Leslie's nervous over-thinking during public speaking.

jokes.text: And if you weren't thinking it, you probably already said it. Political dynasty.
jokes.setup: Leslie introducing herself and her mother as 'the Knopes'
jokes.punchline: She assumes everyone is thinking about political dynasties
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Leslie's dynasty obsession in an inappropriate context.

jokes.text: And others.
jokes.setup: Leslie highlighting Janine Restrepo specifically in her speech
jokes.punchline: Dismisses everyone else in attendance as afterthoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation showing Leslie's transparent political motive.

jokes.text: I love you too, Janine.
jokes.setup: Leslie ending her speech by saying she loves her mom
jokes.punchline: She also declares love for Janine, a stranger
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment showing Leslie's complete lack of social awareness.

jokes.text: This is my evening. I'm Leslie's trophy wife.
jokes.setup: Ann being ignored while Leslie networks
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically embraces the trophy wife role
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's frustration with being marginalized.

jokes.text: And if five years ago you told me I was gonna be in this ballroom with Marlene Griggs-Knope, I would have guessed we were getting married.
jokes.setup: Tom giving an impromptu speech about Marlene
jokes.punchline: Implies he had romantic fantasies about Leslie's mother
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment showing his inappropriate oversharing and attraction to power.

jokes.text: Andy, I can't leave now. I'll get one for you on the way home. Okay, meatball and ham. Wait, is that two different subs? Really? I don't think they can do that.
jokes.setup: Ann at a formal banquet taking a call
jokes.punchline: She's taking Andy's complicated sandwich order during the event
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing how Andy's needs intrude on Ann's rare social outings.

jokes.text: Now, the words 'too sexy' aren't really in my vocabulary. But Marlene, girl, you are too sexy.
jokes.setup: Tom continuing his speech about Marlene
jokes.punchline: Calls the older government official sexy
jokes.explanation: Fits Tom's inappropriate character but not particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: Marlene is a woman. She has worked in the government for three decades. 30 years. Properly applied, that's how long a good varnish should last.
jokes.setup: Ron giving a speech of facts about Marlene
jokes.punchline: Compares her government service to wood varnish durability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson moment - takes mundane facts and makes an absurd woodworking comparison with completely deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: So Marlene, it is true that you have won this award.
jokes.setup: Ron's speech building to a conclusion
jokes.punchline: States the obvious fact as if it's a revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan delivery of something completely obvious as a speech conclusion.

jokes.text: It's 9:30 on a Friday night in Pawnee. There's not gonna be a later, Mark. Come on, now.
jokes.setup: Mark suggesting they could leave later for the bar
jokes.punchline: Tom points out the reality of small-town nightlife
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about small-town limitations that rings true.

jokes.text: Look, I would love to come hit on chicks with you guys, but, uh, she seems kind of engaged in something and I--I think I should probably stay.
jokes.setup: Tom inviting Mark to go hit on women
jokes.punchline: Mark chooses to stay with Ann instead
jokes.explanation: Shows Mark's growing interest in Ann but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: You hit on chicks, for real?
jokes.setup: Mark talking about going to hit on women
jokes.punchline: Ann's incredulous reaction to the crude phrasing
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that shows Ann's more refined sensibilities.

jokes.text: Did she say call her or call her secretary?
jokes.setup: Leslie excitedly reporting her success with Janine
jokes.punchline: Marlene immediately identifies the key detail Leslie missed
jokes.explanation: Shows Marlene's political savvy and Leslie's naivety effectively.

jokes.text: Didn't want to have to use this, but her husband got a D.U.I. in Illinois last week. And she's trying to keep it quiet.
jokes.setup: Marlene explaining they need different tactics with Janine
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of blackmail material
jokes.explanation: Shows Marlene's ruthless political instincts and how casually she handles sensitive information.

jokes.text: Sweetheart, they only give lifetime achievement awards to people who do things like that. Those are eight of the nastiest, most diabolical people you could ever wanna meet.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying she wants to win a Tellenson Award someday
jokes.punchline: Marlene reveals the award winners are all corrupt
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation that recontextualizes the entire event and Leslie's aspirations. Changes audience perspective.

jokes.text: Tony Tellenson was the worst. He tried to re-segregate the drinking fountains.
jokes.setup: Marlene listing corrupt award winners
jokes.punchline: The award's namesake tried to bring back segregation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation that makes the award completely morally bankrupt. Dark but effective humor.

jokes.text: She has a husband who likes to vroom and glug glug glug glug glug.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining Janine's situation to Ann
jokes.punchline: Childish sound effects for drunk driving
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's inability to speak directly about serious topics, but feels forced.

jokes.text: Look, nursing is easy. Yeah. You just go to work and people come in and you heal them.
jokes.setup: Leslie dismissing Ann's criticism of her political tactics
jokes.punchline: Completely oversimplifies the medical profession
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's self-importance and ignorance about other professions.

jokes.text: Make any pancakes lately?
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann arguing about following others' advice
jokes.punchline: Leslie references Ann making pancakes for Andy
jokes.explanation: Callback to pilot episode that shows Leslie can be cutting when defensive.

jokes.text: Yeah. And he's got three crutches. And one of them is you. And the other two are crutches.
jokes.setup: Ann defending Andy's broken legs
jokes.punchline: Leslie calls Ann a crutch then clarifies the math awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Leslie being mean-spirited but in a characteristically awkward way.

jokes.text: It's okay. You're allowed to fight. You two are just like everyone else.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann having their argument
jokes.punchline: Frank still thinks they're a couple having relationship issues
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the running gag with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Cool Ranch, got it. Anything else?
jokes.setup: Ann leaving the formal banquet early
jokes.punchline: She's taking Andy's snack requests over the phone
jokes.explanation: Shows how Andy's demands follow Ann everywhere, even formal events.

jokes.text: He's handsome, I'm a cutie pie, he's laid back, I'm more in your face, but in a fun way. Ladies don't stand a chance.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he and Mark make a good team
jokes.punchline: Tom's completely unself-aware description of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment showing his complete lack of self-awareness about his appeal.

jokes.text: It's called peacocking. Basically, I'm wearing something that kind of makes me stand out, like a peacock. So girls'll be like, 'Hey, what's with that hat?'
jokes.setup: Mark asking about Tom's unusual hat
jokes.punchline: Tom explains pick-up artist terminology with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's adoption of dating strategies that don't suit him at all.

jokes.text: Thank you, Tony, for those inspiring twitches and blinks.
jokes.setup: Tony Tellenson's video message from his hospital bed
jokes.punchline: MC treats incoherent movements as meaningful communication
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about treating a vegetative person's involuntary movements as inspirational.

jokes.text: I know that your husband is a drunk driver. My mom told me.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to be subtle about the blackmail
jokes.punchline: She blurts out the sensitive information directly and cites her source
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - completely fails at subtlety and even reveals her source.

jokes.text: One time I forgot my keys to one of the houses.
jokes.setup: Mark asking for interesting real estate stories
jokes.punchline: Extremely mundane anecdote delivered as if it's fascinating
jokes.explanation: Shows the boring reality of small-town social interactions that Mark is trying to escape.

jokes.text: Look at these guys-- the key-forgetting twins!
jokes.setup: The women bonding over forgetting keys
jokes.punchline: Tom's forced enthusiasm for their mundane connection
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom's desperation to keep the conversation going despite how boring it is.

jokes.text: If there was a Tellenson Award for hooking up with trashy chicks all the time, you'd have several of those awards. You're the king. You're my hero.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to convince Mark to help him with the women
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment comparing promiscuity to civic achievement
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ties Mark's reputation to the episode's award theme.

jokes.text: There was definitely some truth about that thing you said about me and Andy.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann making up after their fight
jokes.punchline: Ann admits Leslie was right about Andy being too dependent
jokes.explanation: Character growth moment that's more touching than funny.

jokes.text: Oh. Hi, Leslie. I thought you were a dude.
jokes.setup: Leslie arriving at Ann's apartment
jokes.punchline: Andy mistakes Leslie for a man because of her haircut
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback that pays off the haircut gag from earlier.

jokes.text: He lives in Florida. In a cemetery.
jokes.setup: Leslie saying her dad was always happy and lives in Florida
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's dead in a characteristically awkward way
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with Leslie's characteristic inability to deliver sensitive information smoothly.

jokes.text: Fondly.
jokes.setup: Leslie mentioning the 'Iron [bleep] of Pawnee' nickname
jokes.punchline: Single word clarification that the profane nickname is meant affectionately
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with excellent comedic timing and economy - one word recontextualizes the insult.

jokes.text: The fact that I haven't even gotten close to cheating on her is a disappointment to both of us.
jokes.setup: Tom talking about being appealing to ladies as a politician
jokes.punchline: He and his wife are disappointed by his fidelity
jokes.explanation: Funny subversion of expected marital dynamics that fits Tom's character perfectly.
